changed.from: In the smallest CS-hedge with \(P_L=(b_L\to y)\), \(P_R=(b_R\to y)\), \(e_\star=(b_L\leftrightarrow b_R)\), \(d\to y\), and a supplied state \(u\) satisfying \(T_L=\{b_L\}\subseteq J(u)\) and \(d\notin J(u)\), the exact supplied law is \(p_{M_\kappa}(X_{b_L},X_{b_R},X_y\mid S=u)=\mathbf 1\{X_{b_L}=z_{b_L}(u)\}\,2^{-1}\) on its feasible values, independently of \(\kappa\). Under \(\operatorname{do}(d=1)\) and \(S=\emptyset\), \(p_{M_\kappa}(X_y=0\mid\operatorname{do}(d=1),S=\emptyset)=(1+\kappa)/2\).
changed.to:
In the smallest CS-hedge with \(P_L=(b_L	o y)\), \(P_R=(b_R	o y)\), \(e_\star=(b_L\leftrightarrow b_R)\), and \(d	o y\), let \(u\) be a supplied state satisfying \(T_L=\{b_L\}\subseteq J(u)\), \(d
otin J(u)\), and \(b_R
otin J(u)\). Put \(c=z_{b_L}(u)\) and define \(h_u(r)=z_y(u)\) if \(y\in J(u)\), while \(h_u(r)=c\oplus r\) if \(y
otin J(u)\). Then the exact supplied law is \[p_{M_\kappa}(X_{b_L}=x_L,X_{b_R}=x_R,X_y=x_y\mid S=u)= rac12\mathbf 1\{x_L=c\}\mathbf 1\{x_y=h_u(x_R)\},\] independently of \(\kappa\). Under \(\operatorname{do}(d=1)\) and \(S=\emptyset\), \(p_{M_\kappa}(X_y=0\mid\operatorname{do}(d=1),S=\emptyset)=(1+\kappa)/2\).
changed.reason: The current hypotheses allow \(b_R\in J(u)\). If both arms are seriously assigned, then \(X_{b_R}\) is fixed and the unique feasible triple can have probability one, contradicting the asserted mass \(1/2\). Requiring the opposite source to be unassigned is the necessary regime restriction; the displayed indicator makes the phrase ``feasible values'' exact.

changed.from: If Open-ended Question 1 has an affirmative answer, then, on finite binary label-coherent CS-SCM inputs satisfying the stated selector-support conditions, \(\mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}(\mathcal I)\) succeeds if and only if \(Q(x_Y)=p(X_Y(a)=x_Y,S=\emptyset)\) is identifiable from \(\{p(X_{V^\circ}\mid S=u):u\in\mathcal U\}\).
changed.to: If Open-ended Question 1 has an affirmative answer, then, on finite binary label-coherent CS-SCM inputs satisfying the stated selector-support conditions and with the target selector mass \(\pi(\emptyset)\) supplied, \(\mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}(\mathcal I)\) succeeds if and only if \(Q(x_Y)=p(X_Y(a)=x_Y,S=\emptyset)\) is identifiable from \(\pi(\emptyset)\) together with \(\{p(X_{V^\circ}\mid S=u):u\in\mathcal U\}\).
changed.reason: The target is a subprobability law and factors as \(Q(x_Y)=\pi(\emptyset)p(X_Y(a)=x_Y\mid S=\emptyset)\). Conditional context laws do not determine \(\pi(\emptyset)\): two selector laws can have the same supplied conditionals and different positive target masses. The original identification claim therefore lacks an observed input required even to normalize its target. Supplying \(\pi(\emptyset)\) narrows the data configuration to the one in which the stated frontier can be meaningful.

note: D0.5 round-0 fail: target mismatch, underdefined selector domain, vacuous frontier, and below-field surviving lemma
directive:
Re-derive the paper from the exact Lee–Ghassami–Shpitser SS-ID query and terminal cs_fail_2 trace. The current core is not salvageable as written. Mandatory constraints:

1. TARGET SEMANTICS: SS-ID targets the context-setting intervention p(X_Y(a,S=∅)), not the natural joint event p(X_Y(a),S=∅). Replace def:target-law and every consumer accordingly. Do not introduce π(∅) or a conditioning factor unless the published algorithm explicitly observes it and the theorem states that altered input; the countermodels must disagree on the interventional target while agreeing on every supplied law.

2. SELECTOR DOMAIN: make J(s) and z_v(s) well-defined for every selector state used by the CS-SCM, or explicitly restrict the model's selector support in a way faithful to the published input. No structural equation may evaluate an undefined override.

3. NONVACUOUS COMPLETE CERTIFICATE: discard the frozen two-arm definition if it cannot cover every exact cs_fail_2 trace. Replace def:cs-hedge by a graph-only label-set-ADMG certificate broad enough to include arbitrary treatment polarity and minimal terminal traces (a context-indexed thicket / response-function null-direction certificate is admissible only if its membership is graph-readable and finite). Prove from the exact Algorithm-2 failure invariants that every cs_fail_2 trace contains the certificate, then prove a uniform paired-model/context-marginal lemma for that certificate. The old counterexample merely refutes the too-narrow class; do not present that strawman refutation as the research contribution.

4. NO VACUITY OR EXPLOSION: delete thm:cs-fail-2-without-cs-hedge and the explosion proof of thm:ss-id-completeness-frontier. A completeness theorem may remain only if its antecedent is substantively proved and its conclusion follows using published soundness plus nonidentification at every failure return. Never claim completeness from a refuted antecedent.

5. FIELD-TIER DELTA: state and prove the precise theorem-level difference from (i) ordinary Shpitser–Pearl hedges, (ii) Lee–Correa–Bareinboim hedgelets/thickets, and (iii) the in-repository eid_data_fusion_k_source overlap-hedge conjecture. The context-marginal calculation alone is below the requested field floor without terminal-trace extraction.

6. TRUTHFUL EXIT: verify every claimed cs_fail_2 trace against the actual pseudocode and graph semantics. If the terminal extraction theorem cannot be proved, return a genuine open obligation rather than a conditional-vacuous theorem or manufactured counterexample. Preserve the useful parity/context-marginal lemma only as supporting content, not as the headline.

changed.from: For every \(\mathfrak H\in\mathcal H_{\mathrm{CS}}(\bar G,A,a,Y,\mathcal U)\) and \(\kappa\in(-1,1)\), \(M_\kappa\) is a finite CS-SCM compatible with \(\bar G\). The selector, the edge-local source \(U_\star\), and all remaining degenerate exogenous sources are mutually independent, and \(U_\star\) is a parent only of the endpoints of \(e_\star\). For every \(u\in\mathcal U\), \(P_{u,\kappa}\) is therefore a well-defined conditional law.
changed.to: For every two-arm parity-severing configuration in the domain of \(M_\kappa=\operatorname{CSParity}(\bar G,d,y,b_L,b_R,P_L,P_R,e_\star,\pi,\kappa)\) and every \(\kappa\in(-1,1)\), \(M_\kappa\) is a finite binary CS-SCM compatible with \(\bar G\). The selector, the edge-local source \(U_\star=(B_L,B_R)\), and all remaining degenerate exogenous sources are mutually independent, and \(U_\star\) is a parent only of the endpoints of \(e_\star\). The structural equations are defined for every \(s\in\mathcal S\), and \(P_{u,\kappa}=p_{M_\kappa}(X_{V^\circ}\mid S=u)\) is well defined for every \(u\in\mathcal U=\operatorname{supp}(\pi)\).
changed.reason: The old quantifier ranged over a proposed universal CS-hedge class that is not implied by the exact Algorithm 2 terminal trace. The parity construction remains valid only as a supporting two-arm configuration after selector overrides are made total on the entire selector state space.

changed.from: For every \(\mathfrak H\in\mathcal H_{\mathrm{CS}}(\bar G,A,a,Y,\mathcal U)\), every \(u\in\mathcal U\), and every \(\kappa\in(-1,1)\), \(P_{u,\kappa}=P_{u,0}\). More explicitly, if \(T_L\subseteq J(u)\), then the response vector is a function of \(B_R\) and selector-fixed constants only, and \[\Pr_\kappa(B_R=j\mid S=u)=\sum_{i=0}^1\tfrac14[1+\kappa(-1)^{i+j}]=\tfrac12.\] The symmetric identity holds when \(T_R\subseteq J(u)\). In the unsevered target state, \[Q_\kappa(X_y=0)=\pi(\emptyset)\Pr_\kappa(B_L\oplus B_R=0)=\pi(\emptyset)\tfrac{1+\kappa}{2}.\] Hence \(P_{u,1/2}=P_{u,-1/2}\) for all \(u\in\mathcal U\), while \(Q_{1/2}\ne Q_{-1/2}\).
changed.to:
For every two-arm parity-severing configuration in the domain of \(M_\kappa\), every \(u\in\mathcal U=\operatorname{supp}(\pi)\), and every \(\kappa\in(-1,1)\), \(P_{u,\kappa}=P_{u,0}\). If \(T_L\subseteq J(u)\), then the full response vector is a function of \(B_R\) and selector-fixed constants only, and
\[\Pr_\kappa(B_R=j\mid S=u)=\sum_{i=0}^1\frac14\{1+\kappa(-1)^{i+j}\}=\frac12.\]
The symmetric identity holds if \(T_R\subseteq J(u)\). Since the domain requires one of these two inclusions at every supported selector state, the full observed joint laws agree:
\[p_{M_\kappa}(X_{V^\circ}=x,S=s)=p_{M_0}(X_{V^\circ}=x,S=s)\quad\text{for every }(x,s).\]
Under \(\operatorname{do}(A=a,S=\emptyset)\), the parity-gate coordinate satisfies \(X_y=B_L\oplus B_R\), and therefore
\[Q_\kappa(X_y=0)=\Pr_\kappa(B_L\oplus B_R=0)=\frac{1+\kappa}{2}.\]
Consequently \(M_{1/2}\) and \(M_{-1/2}\) agree on the full observed SS-ID input but disagree on the context-setting target.
changed.reason: This is the faithful narrow repair of the useful parity calculation. It removes the spurious factor \(\pi(\emptyset)\), uses the intervention \(\operatorname{do}(S=\emptyset)\), and upgrades equality of selected conditionals to equality of the full observed joint law by exhausting \(\operatorname{supp}(\pi)\). It is retained only as supporting content because the exact terminal trace does not imply its two-arm premise and the published supplementary material already contains a more general selector-aware parity mechanism.

changed.from:
In the smallest CS-hedge with \(P_L=(b_L	o y)\), \(P_R=(b_R	o y)\), \(e_\star=(b_L\leftrightarrow b_R)\), and \(d	o y\), let \(u\) be a supplied state satisfying \(T_L=\{b_L\}\subseteq J(u)\), \(d
otin J(u)\), and \(b_R
otin J(u)\). Put \(c=z_{b_L}(u)\) and define \(h_u(r)=z_y(u)\) if \(y\in J(u)\), while \(h_u(r)=c\oplus r\) if \(y
otin J(u)\). Then the exact supplied law is \[p_{M_\kappa}(X_{b_L}=x_L,X_{b_R}=x_R,X_y=x_y\mid S=u)= rac12\mathbf 1\{x_L=c\}\mathbf 1\{x_y=h_u(x_R)\},\] independently of \(\kappa\). Under \(\operatorname{do}(d=1)\) and \(S=\emptyset\), \(p_{M_\kappa}(X_y=0\mid\operatorname{do}(d=1),S=\emptyset)=(1+\kappa)/2\).
changed.to:
In the smallest two-arm parity-severing configuration with \(P_L=(b_L\to y)\), \(P_R=(b_R\to y)\), \(e_\star=(b_L\leftrightarrow b_R)\), and \(d\to y\), let \(u\in\operatorname{supp}(\pi)\) satisfy \(T_L=\{b_L\}\subseteq J(u)\), \(d\notin J(u)\), and \(b_R\notin J(u)\). Put \(c=z_{b_L}(u)\) and define \(h_u(r)=z_y(u)\) if \(y\in J(u)\), while \(h_u(r)=c\oplus r\) if \(y\notin J(u)\). Then
\[p_{M_\kappa}(X_{b_L}=x_L,X_{b_R}=x_R,X_y=x_y\mid S=u)=\frac12\mathbf 1\{x_L=c\}\mathbf 1\{x_y=h_u(x_R)\},\]
independently of \(\kappa\). Under the context-setting intervention \(\operatorname{do}(d=1,S=\emptyset)\),
\[p_{M_\kappa}(X_y=0\mid\operatorname{do}(d=1,S=\emptyset))=\frac{1+\kappa}{2}.\]
changed.reason: The first calculation remains a useful finite audit, but it is not a terminal completeness certificate. The last display must intervene on \(S\), rather than condition on the natural event \(S=\emptyset\).

changed.from: If Open-ended Question 1 has an affirmative answer, then, on finite binary label-coherent CS-SCM inputs satisfying the stated selector-support conditions and with the target selector mass \(\pi(\emptyset)\) supplied, \(\mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}(\mathcal I)\) succeeds if and only if \(Q(x_Y)=p(X_Y(a)=x_Y,S=\emptyset)\) is identifiable from \(\pi(\emptyset)\) together with \(\{p(X_{V^\circ}\mid S=u):u\in\mathcal U\}\).
changed.to:
On finite binary CS-SCM inputs, \(\mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}\) is sound but not complete. There is a four-vertex LS-ADMMG on \(\{S,A_0,Y_0,W_0\}\), with directed edges \(S\to A_0\to Y_0\) and \(S\to W_0\), an unlabelled edge \(S\leftrightarrow Y_0\), an \(A_0\)-labelled edge copy \(A_0\leftrightarrow Y_0\), and positive selector support on two states that both seriously assign \(A_0=a_0\) and differ only in whether \(W_0\) is seriously assigned, for which the algorithm reaches the final bare failure return of Algorithm 2, while
\[p(X_{Y_0}(a_0,S=\emptyset)=y_0)=p(X_{Y_0}=y_0)\]
for every compatible CS-SCM and every \(y_0\in\{0,1\}\). Thus failure at that return does not imply nonidentification. The published nonidentification conclusions for Algorithm 1 lines 4 and 14 and Algorithm 2 line 3 are unaffected.
changed.reason: The old theorem is conditional on an unresolved antecedent, uses the wrong natural-event target, and assumes an input consisting of conditional laws plus \(\pi(\emptyset)\), whereas the published algorithm takes the full observed joint law. The support-pooling family gives a direct identifiable failure and therefore refutes, rather than proves, the published completeness conjecture.

changed.from: \(J(u)=\{W_\ell:\ell\in u\}\), and \(z_v(u)=z_\ell\) whenever \(v=W_\ell\in J(u)\).
changed.to: For every selector state \(s\in\mathcal S\), define \(J(s)=\{W_\ell:\ell\in s\}\) and, for \(v\in J(s)\), define \(z_v(s)=z_\ell\) for any \(\ell\in s\) satisfying \(W_\ell=v\). Statewise label-value coherence makes \(z_v(s)\) single valued, and \(J(\emptyset)=\emptyset\).
changed.reason: The structural equations evaluate the override at every realized selector state and under \(\operatorname{do}(S=\emptyset)\), not only at supplied indices denoted by \(u\).

changed.from: \(Q_\kappa(x_Y)=p_{M_\kappa}(X_Y(a)=x_Y,S=\emptyset)=\pi(\emptyset)\,p_{M_\kappa}(X_Y=x_Y\mid\operatorname{do}(A=a),S=\emptyset).\)
changed.to: For every compatible CS-SCM \(M\), define the context-setting target law by \(Q_M(x_Y)=p_M(X_Y(a,S=\emptyset)=x_Y)=p_M(X_Y=x_Y\mid\operatorname{do}(A=a,S=\emptyset))\). For the parity model, write \(Q_\kappa=Q_{M_\kappa}\).
changed.reason: SS-ID targets an intervention setting both \(A\) and \(S\). The target is a normalized probability law and contains neither a natural-event factor nor \(\pi(\emptyset)\).

changed.from: \(M_\kappa=\operatorname{CSParity}(\bar G,\mathfrak H,\pi,\kappa):\ S\sim\pi;\ \Pr_\kappa(B_L=i,B_R=j)=\tfrac14[1+\kappa(-1)^{i+j}];\ U_\star=(B_L,B_R)\perp S;\ \text{all other exogenous variables are mutually independent and degenerate};\ X_v=z_v(S)\text{ for }v\in J(S);\ X_{b_L}=B_L\text{ and }X_{b_R}=B_R\text{ when their vertices are not in }J(S);\ X_v=X_w\text{ when }(w\to v)\text{ is the preceding edge of }P_L\text{ or }P_R\text{ and }v\notin J(S)\cup\{y\};\ X_d=1\text{ when }d\notin J(S);\ X_y=z_y(S)\text{ when }y\in J(S),\text{ and otherwise }X_y=X_d(X_{p_L}\oplus X_{p_R}),\text{ where }p_L\to y\text{ and }p_R\to y\text{ are the final edges of }P_L\text{ and }P_R;\ X_v=0\text{ for all remaining unassigned }v.\)
changed.to: The domain of \(M_\kappa=\operatorname{CSParity}(\bar G,d,y,b_L,b_R,P_L,P_R,e_\star,\pi,\kappa)\) consists of tuples for which \(d\in A\), \(a_d=1\), \(y\in Y\), \(b_L\) and \(b_R\) are distinct vertices outside \(A\cup Y\), \(P_L\) and \(P_R\) are directed simple paths from \(b_L\) and \(b_R\) to \(y\) with \(\operatorname{v}(P_L)\cap\operatorname{v}(P_R)=\{y\}\), \(e_\star=(b_L\leftrightarrow b_R)\in E^{\leftrightarrow}\), \(d\to y\in E^{\to}\), \(T_j=\operatorname{v}(P_j)\setminus\{y\}\) for \(j\in\{L,R\}\), \((T_L\cup T_R)\cap A=\emptyset\), and \(T_L\subseteq J(u)\) or \(T_R\subseteq J(u)\) for every \(u\in\operatorname{supp}(\pi)\). For every \(s\in\mathcal S\), first evaluate the laidback equations \(X_{b_L}=B_L\), \(X_{b_R}=B_R\), copying along \(P_L\) and \(P_R\), \(X_d=1\), \(X_y=X_d(X_{p_L}\oplus X_{p_R})\), and \(X_v=0\) at remaining vertices; then replace the equation at every \(v\in J(s)\) by \(X_v=z_v(s)\). Here \(S\sim\pi\), \(U_\star=(B_L,B_R)\perp S\), \(\Pr_\kappa(B_L=i,B_R=j)=\tfrac14[1+\kappa(-1)^{i+j}]\), and all other exogenous sources are mutually independent and degenerate.
changed.reason: This makes the selector override total on \(\mathcal S\) and demotes the construction to its mathematically valid supporting role, independent of any claimed universal terminal hedge.

changed.from: For the terminal label-set ADMG, enumerate pairs of vertex-disjoint directed arms ending at an outcome \(y\) and joined at a bidirected edge \(e_\star\); compute \(J(u)\) from the child-indexed labels in each supplied state \(u\), and retain exactly the pairs for which \(T_L\subseteq J(u)\) or \(T_R\subseteq J(u)\) for every \(u\in\mathcal U\).
changed.to: First enumerate the finite canonical binary response maps compatible with \(\bar G\), one finite independent exogenous source for every bidirected edge copy, and one finite independent local source for every observed vertex. For each distinguished edge source \(U_\star\), form the finite observed and target kernels \(K_{\mathrm{obs}}(x,s\mid t)\) and \(K_Q(x_Y\mid t)\), and solve the explicit semialgebraic feasibility system in \(\lambda_0\), \(h\), and the remaining source probabilities given in the response-null certificate definition. A feasible solution is a response-null certificate. Independently compute \(R=\operatorname{an}_{\bar G^{[\emptyset]}_{V^\circ\setminus A}}(Y)\) and accept a support-pooling identification certificate when, for every \(s\in\operatorname{supp}(\pi)\), \(A\subseteq J(s)\), \((z_v(s))_{v\in A}=a\), and \(J(s)\cap R=\emptyset\), and when \(S\notin\operatorname{an}_{\bar G^{[\emptyset]}_{V\setminus A}}(R)\). All response enumerations and polynomial systems are finite, and the graph tests use support enumeration, ancestry, districts, and fixing reachability.
changed.reason: The old arm enumerator is not implied by the terminal trace. The corrected handle makes the broad response-null certificate mechanically checkable and separately detects the support-pooling pattern that yields an identifying functional at a terminal failure.

changed.from: {"id":"def:cs-hedge","name":"Context-specific hedge","construction":"\\(\\mathcal H_{\\mathrm{CS}}(\\bar G,A,a,Y,\\mathcal U)=\\{\\mathfrak H=(d,y,b_L,b_R,P_L,P_R,e_\\star):d\\in A,\\ a_d=1,\\ y\\in Y,\\ P_L\\text{ and }P_R\\text{ are directed simple paths in }\\bar G\\text{ from }b_L\\text{ and }b_R\\text{ to }y,\\ \\operatorname{v}(P_L)\\cap\\operatorname{v}(P_R)=\\{y\\},\\ e_\\star=(b_L\\leftrightarrow b_R)\\in E^{\\leftrightarrow},\\ (d\\to y)\\in E^{\\to},\\ T_j=\\operatorname{v}(P_j)\\setminus\\{y\\}\\text{ for }j\\in\\{L,R\\},\\ (T_L\\cup T_R)\\cap A=\\emptyset, \\forall u\\in\\mathcal U\\,[T_L\\subseteq J(u)\\ \\vee\\ T_R\\subseteq J(u)]\\}.\\)","by_member_properties":["ass:finite-binary-domain","ass:recursive-directed-part","ass:label-value-coherence"]}
changed.to: {"id":"def:cs-hedge","name":"Context-specific response-null certificate","construction":"For a finite binary LS-ADMMG \\(\\bar G\\), let \\(\\mathcal H_{\\mathrm{CS}}(\\bar G,A,a,Y,\\mathcal U)\\) be the set of tuples \\(\\mathfrak H=(e_\\star,\\Omega_\\star,\\lambda_0,h,\\mathbf f,\\rho)\\) with the following properties. The tuple \\((\\mathbf f,\\rho)\\) specifies a finite canonical CS-SCM skeleton compatible with \\(\\bar G\\): there is one mutually independent exogenous source for each bidirected edge copy, parent only of that copy's endpoints, one mutually independent finite local source for each observed vertex, deterministic structural maps of the directed parents, and the serious-selector override at every child of \\(S\\). Every source state space is the graph-determined finite set of deterministic response types of its children. The distinguished source \\(U_\\star\\) belongs to edge copy \\(e_\\star\\), its graph-determined state space is \\(\\Omega_\\star\\), and all other source probabilities are collected in \\(\\rho\\). The vector \\(\\lambda_0\\) is a probability mass function on \\(\\Omega_\\star\\), \\(\\sum_{t\\in\\Omega_\\star}h(t)=0\\), and \\(\\lambda_0(t)>|h(t)|\\) for every \\(t\\). Holding \\((\\mathbf f,\\rho)\\) fixed, define \\(K_{\\mathrm{obs}}(x,s\\mid t)=\\Pr_{\\mathbf f,\\rho}(X_{V^\\circ}=x,S=s\\mid U_\\star=t)\\) and \\(K_Q(x_Y\\mid t)=\\Pr_{\\mathbf f,\\rho}(X_Y(a,S=\\emptyset)=x_Y\\mid U_\\star=t)\\) by summing the finite product law of all other sources. Finally require \\(\\sum_t h(t)K_{\\mathrm{obs}}(x,s\\mid t)=0\\) for every \\((x,s)\\), and require \\(\\sum_t h(t)K_Q(x_Y\\mid t)\\ne0\\) for at least one \\(x_Y\\).","inputs":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(A\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(Y\\)","\\(\\mathcal U\\)"]}
changed.reason: The frozen two-arm object is not extracted by the exact line-9 invariant. This replacement is a broad finite, graph-readable response-function null-direction certificate that permits arbitrary treatment polarity, selector-incident confounding, and minimal terminal graphs. Its soundness is an algebraic paired-model lemma; the identifiable support-pooling line-9 family shows that such a certificate cannot exist at every terminal return.

changed.from: {"id":"def:cs-fail-2-class","name":"Uncovered SS-ID failure class","construction":"\\(\\mathcal C_{\\mathrm{cs2}}=\\{\\mathcal I:\\mathcal I\\text{ is a finite SS-ID input and Algorithm 2 of }\\mathsf{SS\\text{-}ID}\\text{ reaches its remaining terminal return }\\mathtt{cs\\_fail\\_2},\\text{ rather than the line 3 hedge return covered by Theorem 5 of Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser}\\}.\\)","by_member_properties":["ass:finite-binary-domain"]}
changed.to: {"id":"def:cs-fail-2-class","name":"Uncovered SS-ID final failure class","construction":"\\(\\mathcal C_{\\mathrm{cs2}}\\) is the class of finite SS-ID inputs for which Algorithm 1 calls Algorithm 2 on a district \\(D^\\ast\\) with \\(S\\in C=\\operatorname{cl}(D^\\ast)\\) and \\(\\operatorname{ch}^\\ast(S)=\\operatorname{ch}(S)\\cap(C\\setminus D^\\ast)\\ne\\emptyset\\), and for which every supported selector state \\(s\\) that is laidback for \\(D^\\ast\\), serious for a subset of \\(\\operatorname{ch}^\\ast(S)\\), and consistent with \\(a_{\\operatorname{spa}(D^\\ast)}\\) fails at least one of Algorithm 2 line 7's graphical independence and reachability conditions. Equivalently, the loop exhausts all candidates and reaches the final bare failure return at line 9.","by_member_properties":["ass:finite-binary-domain","ass:supplied-context-mass"]}
changed.reason: This is the exact published terminal invariant. It is a universal disjunction of an independence failure or a reachability failure, not a whole-arm-severance condition.

changed.from: {"id":"def:serious-assignment","name":"Serious selector assignment","construction":"For every selector state \\(s\\in\\mathcal S\\), define \\(J(s)=\\{W_\\ell:\\ell\\in s\\}\\) and, for \\(v\\in J(s)\\), define \\(z_v(s)=z_\\ell\\) for any \\(\\ell\\in s\\) satisfying \\(W_\\ell=v\\). Statewise label-value coherence makes \\(z_v(s)\\) single valued, and \\(J(\\emptyset)=\\emptyset\\).","inputs":["\\(u\\)","\\(W_\\ell\\)","\\(z_\\ell\\)"]}
changed.to: {"id":"def:serious-assignment","name":"Serious selector assignment","construction":"For every selector state \\(s\\in\\mathcal S\\), define \\(J(s)=\\{W_\\ell:\\ell\\in s\\}\\) and, for \\(v\\in J(s)\\), define \\(z_v(s)=z_\\ell\\) for any \\(\\ell\\in s\\) satisfying \\(W_\\ell=v\\). Statewise label-value coherence makes \\(z_v(s)\\) single valued, and \\(J(\\emptyset)=\\emptyset\\).","inputs":["\\(\\mathcal S\\)","\\(W_\\ell\\)","\\(z_\\ell\\)"]}
changed.reason: The selector override must be total on the full state space, and the structured input list must no longer restrict the definition to a supplied context \(u\).

changed.from: {"id":"def:target-law","name":"Natural-selector target law","construction":"For every compatible CS-SCM \\(M\\), define the context-setting target law by \\(Q_M(x_Y)=p_M(X_Y(a,S=\\emptyset)=x_Y)=p_M(X_Y=x_Y\\mid\\operatorname{do}(A=a,S=\\emptyset))\\). For the parity model, write \\(Q_\\kappa=Q_{M_\\kappa}\\).","inputs":["\\(M_\\kappa\\)","\\(X_Y\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(\\pi\\)","\\(\\emptyset\\)"]}
changed.to: {"id":"def:target-law","name":"Context-setting interventional target law","construction":"For every compatible CS-SCM \\(M\\), define \\(Q_M(x_Y)=p_M(X_Y(a,S=\\emptyset)=x_Y)=p_M(X_Y=x_Y\\mid\\operatorname{do}(A=a,S=\\emptyset))\\). For the parity model, write \\(Q_\\kappa=Q_{M_\\kappa}\\).","inputs":["\\(M_\\kappa\\)","\\(X_Y\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(\\emptyset\\)"]}
changed.reason: The full replacement removes \(\pi\) from the structured input list as well as from the formula.

changed.from: {"id":"def:parity-cs-scm","name":"Selector-aware parity CS-SCM","construction":"The domain of \\(M_\\kappa=\\operatorname{CSParity}(\\bar G,d,y,b_L,b_R,P_L,P_R,e_\\star,\\pi,\\kappa)\\) consists of tuples for which \\(d\\in A\\), \\(a_d=1\\), \\(y\\in Y\\), \\(b_L\\) and \\(b_R\\) are distinct vertices outside \\(A\\cup Y\\), \\(P_L\\) and \\(P_R\\) are directed simple paths from \\(b_L\\) and \\(b_R\\) to \\(y\\) with \\(\\operatorname{v}(P_L)\\cap\\operatorname{v}(P_R)=\\{y\\}\\), \\(e_\\star=(b_L\\leftrightarrow b_R)\\in E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\), \\(d\\to y\\in E^{\\to}\\), \\(T_j=\\operatorname{v}(P_j)\\setminus\\{y\\}\\) for \\(j\\in\\{L,R\\}\\), \\((T_L\\cup T_R)\\cap A=\\emptyset\\), and \\(T_L\\subseteq J(u)\\) or \\(T_R\\subseteq J(u)\\) for every \\(u\\in\\operatorname{supp}(\\pi)\\). For every \\(s\\in\\mathcal S\\), first evaluate the laidback equations \\(X_{b_L}=B_L\\), \\(X_{b_R}=B_R\\), copying along \\(P_L\\) and \\(P_R\\), \\(X_d=1\\), \\(X_y=X_d(X_{p_L}\\oplus X_{p_R})\\), and \\(X_v=0\\) at remaining vertices; then replace the equation at every \\(v\\in J(s)\\) by \\(X_v=z_v(s)\\). Here \\(S\\sim\\pi\\), \\(U_\\star=(B_L,B_R)\\perp S\\), \\(\\Pr_\\kappa(B_L=i,B_R=j)=\\tfrac14[1+\\kappa(-1)^{i+j}]\\), and all other exogenous sources are mutually independent and degenerate.","inputs":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(\\mathfrak H\\)","\\(\\pi\\)","\\(\\kappa\\)","\\(J(u)\\)","\\(z_v(u)\\)"]}
changed.to: {"id":"def:parity-cs-scm","name":"Two-arm selector-aware parity CS-SCM","construction":"The domain of \\(M_\\kappa=\\operatorname{CSParity}(\\bar G,d,y,b_L,b_R,P_L,P_R,e_\\star,\\pi,\\kappa)\\) consists of tuples for which \\(d\\in A\\), \\(a_d=1\\), \\(y\\in Y\\), \\(b_L\\) and \\(b_R\\) are distinct vertices outside \\(A\\cup Y\\), \\(P_L\\) and \\(P_R\\) are directed simple paths from \\(b_L\\) and \\(b_R\\) to \\(y\\) with \\(\\operatorname{v}(P_L)\\cap\\operatorname{v}(P_R)=\\{y\\}\\), \\(e_\\star=(b_L\\leftrightarrow b_R)\\in E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\), \\(d\\to y\\in E^{\\to}\\), \\(T_j=\\operatorname{v}(P_j)\\setminus\\{y\\}\\) for \\(j\\in\\{L,R\\}\\), \\((T_L\\cup T_R)\\cap A=\\emptyset\\), and \\(T_L\\subseteq J(u)\\) or \\(T_R\\subseteq J(u)\\) for every \\(u\\in\\operatorname{supp}(\\pi)\\). For every \\(s\\in\\mathcal S\\), first evaluate the laidback equations \\(X_{b_L}=B_L\\), \\(X_{b_R}=B_R\\), copying along \\(P_L\\) and \\(P_R\\), \\(X_d=1\\), \\(X_y=X_d(X_{p_L}\\oplus X_{p_R})\\), and \\(X_v=0\\) at remaining vertices; then replace the equation at every \\(v\\in J(s)\\) by \\(X_v=z_v(s)\\). Here \\(S\\sim\\pi\\), \\(U_\\star=(B_L,B_R)\\perp S\\), \\(\\Pr_\\kappa(B_L=i,B_R=j)=\\tfrac14[1+\\kappa(-1)^{i+j}]\\), and all other exogenous sources are mutually independent and degenerate.","inputs":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(d\\)","\\(y\\)","\\(b_L,b_R\\)","\\(P_L,P_R\\)","\\(e_\\star\\)","\\(\\pi\\)","\\(\\kappa\\)","\\(J(u)\\)","\\(z_v(u)\\)"]}
changed.reason: The full replacement removes the obsolete response-null certificate tuple from the parity construction's inputs and states its narrow domain explicitly.

changed.from: {"id":"def:cs-hedge-extraction-handle","name":"Terminal-trace extraction handle","construction":"First enumerate the finite canonical binary response maps compatible with \\(\\bar G\\), one finite independent exogenous source for every bidirected edge copy, and one finite independent local source for every observed vertex. For each distinguished edge source \\(U_\\star\\), form the finite observed and target kernels \\(K_{\\mathrm{obs}}(x,s\\mid t)\\) and \\(K_Q(x_Y\\mid t)\\), and solve the explicit semialgebraic feasibility system in \\(\\lambda_0\\), \\(h\\), and the remaining source probabilities given in the response-null certificate definition. A feasible solution is a response-null certificate. Independently compute \\(R=\\operatorname{an}_{\\bar G^{[\\emptyset]}_{V^\\circ\\setminus A}}(Y)\\) and accept a support-pooling identification certificate when, for every \\(s\\in\\operatorname{supp}(\\pi)\\), \\(A\\subseteq J(s)\\), \\((z_v(s))_{v\\in A}=a\\), and \\(J(s)\\cap R=\\emptyset\\), and when \\(S\\notin\\operatorname{an}_{\\bar G^{[\\emptyset]}_{V\\setminus A}}(R)\\). All response enumerations and polynomial systems are finite, and the graph tests use support enumeration, ancestry, districts, and fixing reachability.","inputs":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(\\mathcal U\\)","\\(J(u)\\)","\\(A\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(Y\\)"]}
changed.to: {"id":"def:cs-hedge-extraction-handle","name":"Response-null and support-pooling checks","construction":"First enumerate the finite canonical binary response maps compatible with \\(\\bar G\\), one finite independent exogenous source for every bidirected edge copy, and one finite independent local source for every observed vertex. For each distinguished edge source \\(U_\\star\\), form \\(K_{\\mathrm{obs}}(x,s\\mid t)\\) and \\(K_Q(x_Y\\mid t)\\), and solve the explicit semialgebraic feasibility system in \\(\\lambda_0\\), \\(h\\), and the remaining source probabilities given in the response-null certificate definition. Independently compute \\(R=\\operatorname{an}_{\\bar G^{[\\emptyset]}_{V^\\circ\\setminus A}}(Y)\\) and accept a support-pooling identification certificate when, for every \\(s\\in\\operatorname{supp}(\\pi)\\), \\(A\\subseteq J(s)\\), \\((z_v(s))_{v\\in A}=a\\), and \\(J(s)\\cap R=\\emptyset\\), and when \\(S\\notin\\operatorname{an}_{\\bar G^{[\\emptyset]}_{V\\setminus A}}(R)\\).","inputs":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(\\pi\\)","\\(J(u)\\)","\\(A\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(Y\\)"]}
changed.reason: The complete replacement synchronizes the new finite response-null feasibility problem and the support-pooling identification test with their structured inputs.

changed.from: {"id":"ass:recursive-directed-part","kind":"graphical","condition":"\\((V^\\circ,E^{\\to})\\) is acyclic.","free_symbols":["\\(V^\\circ\\)","\\(E^{\\to}\\)"],"standard":{"name":"recursive semi-Markovian acyclicity","cite":"ShpitserPearl2006Joint"},"used_by":["def:cs-hedge","def:parity-cs-scm","lem:parity-cs-scm-validity"]}
changed.to: {"id":"ass:recursive-directed-part","kind":"graphical","condition":"The directed part of \\(\\bar G\\) on \\(V\\), including selector-incident edges, is acyclic.","free_symbols":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(V\\)"],"standard":{"name":"recursive semi-Markovian acyclicity","cite":"ShpitserPearl2006Joint"},"used_by":["def:parity-cs-scm","lem:parity-cs-scm-validity","thm:context-marginal-sufficiency"]}
changed.reason: The exact LS-ADMMG contains directed edges incident to the selector; acyclicity only on \(V^\circ\) is insufficient.

changed.from: {"id":"ass:label-value-coherence","kind":"model","condition":"\\(W_\\ell=W_{\\ell'}\\Rightarrow z_\\ell=z_{\\ell'}\\) for all \\(\\ell,\\ell'\\in\\mathcal L\\).","free_symbols":["\\(W_\\ell\\)","\\(W_{\\ell'}\\)","\\(z_\\ell\\)","\\(z_{\\ell'}\\)","\\(\\mathcal L\\)"],"novel":{"flag":true,"justification":"A selector state can name several labels for one child only when they impose the same value. This records the operational consistency of simultaneous encouragement assignments and makes \\(J(u)\\) and the override equation unambiguous."},"used_by":["def:serious-assignment","def:parity-cs-scm","lem:parity-cs-scm-validity"]}
changed.to: {"id":"ass:label-value-coherence","kind":"model","condition":"For every \\(s\\in\\mathcal S\\) and every \\(\\ell,\\ell'\\in s\\), \\(W_\\ell=W_{\\ell'}\\Rightarrow z_\\ell=z_{\\ell'}\\).","free_symbols":["\\(\\mathcal S\\)","\\(W_\\ell\\)","\\(W_{\\ell'}\\)","\\(z_\\ell\\)","\\(z_{\\ell'}\\)"],"novel":{"flag":true,"justification":"This is the coherent-subset encoding of the child-specific intervention-indicator and intervention-value selector in a binary CS-SCM. It permits different contexts to assign different values to the same child, while forbidding contradictory assignments within one context."},"used_by":["def:serious-assignment","def:parity-cs-scm","lem:parity-cs-scm-validity","thm:context-marginal-sufficiency"]}
changed.reason: The frozen global implication incorrectly forbids two distinct contexts from assigning different values to the same child.

changed.from: {"id":"ass:supplied-context-mass","kind":"data","condition":"\\(\\pi(u)>0\\) for every \\(u\\in\\mathcal U\\).","free_symbols":["\\(\\pi\\)","\\(u\\)","\\(\\mathcal U\\)"],"standard":{"name":"positive supplied context probability","cite":"LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic"},"used_by":["def:parity-cs-scm","lem:parity-cs-scm-validity","thm:context-marginal-sufficiency"]}
changed.to: {"id":"ass:supplied-context-mass","kind":"data","condition":"\\(\\mathcal U=\\operatorname{supp}(\\pi)=\\{s\\in\\mathcal S:\\pi(s)>0\\}\\).","free_symbols":["\\(\\mathcal U\\)","\\(\\pi\\)","\\(\\mathcal S\\)"],"standard":{"name":"exhaustive positive selector support","cite":"LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic"},"used_by":["def:cs-fail-2-class","def:cs-hedge-extraction-handle","lem:parity-cs-scm-validity","thm:context-marginal-sufficiency","prop:normalization-sanity-reduction"]}
changed.reason: SS-ID takes the full observed joint law \(p(V)\), so every positive-support selector state must be included; an arbitrary subset of conditionals is not the published input.

changed.from: {"id":"ass:target-context-mass","kind":"data","condition":"\\(\\pi(\\emptyset)>0\\).","free_symbols":["\\(\\pi\\)","\\(\\emptyset\\)"],"standard":{"name":"positive target-context probability","cite":"LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic"},"used_by":["def:parity-cs-scm","thm:context-marginal-sufficiency"]}
changed.to: {"id":"ass:target-context-mass","kind":"model","condition":"\\(\\emptyset\\in\\mathcal S\\).","free_symbols":["\\(\\emptyset\\)","\\(\\mathcal S\\)"],"standard":{"name":"admissible laidback intervention value","cite":"LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic"},"used_by":["def:target-law","thm:context-marginal-sufficiency","prop:normalization-sanity-reduction"]}
changed.reason: The target value must be an admissible intervention value, but the published problem neither requires nor generally has \(\pi(\emptyset)>0\).

changed.from: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal S\\)","type":"finite set","def":"\\(\\mathcal S=2^{\\mathcal L}\\).","role":"selector state space","refs":["\\(\\mathcal L\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal S\\)","type":"finite set","def":"\\(\\mathcal S=\\{s\\subseteq\\mathcal L:\\ell,\\ell'\\in s,\\ W_\\ell=W_{\\ell'}\\Rightarrow z_\\ell=z_{\\ell'}\\}\\).","role":"coherent selector state space","refs":["\\(\\mathcal L\\)","\\(W_\\ell\\)","\\(z_\\ell\\)"]}
changed.reason: This is the binary coherent-subset encoding of the published child-specific selector pairs and defines every state on which an override may be evaluated.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal U\\)","type":"finite subset of \\(\\mathcal S\\setminus\\{\\emptyset\\}\\)","def":"Selector states for which conditional laws are supplied.","role":"supplied contexts","refs":["\\(\\mathcal S\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal U\\)","type":"finite subset of \\(\\mathcal S\\)","def":"\\(\\mathcal U=\\operatorname{supp}(\\pi)\\).","role":"positive selector support","refs":["\\(\\mathcal S\\)","\\(\\pi\\)"]}
changed.reason: The algorithm observes the coherent joint law on the entire positive support of \(S\).

changed.from: {"name":"\\(E^{\\to}\\)","type":"finite set","def":"Directed edge copies on \\(V^\\circ\\).","role":"directed edges","refs":["\\(V^\\circ\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(E^{\\to}\\)","type":"finite set","def":"Directed edge copies on \\(V\\), including edges incident to \\(S\\); each copy may carry a finite child-label set.","role":"labelled directed edges","refs":["\\(V\\)","\\(S\\)"]}
changed.reason: The terminal trace depends essentially on selector-to-child directed edges.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\)","type":"finite set","def":"Bidirected edge copies on \\(V^\\circ\\).","role":"bidirected edges","refs":["\\(V^\\circ\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\)","type":"finite set","def":"Bidirected edge copies on \\(V\\), including selector-incident copies; each copy may carry a finite child-label set.","role":"labelled bidirected edges","refs":["\\(V\\)","\\(S\\)"]}
changed.reason: The exact line-9 independence failure can be caused by an unlabelled selector-outcome bidirected edge.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(\\bar G\\)","type":"label-set ADMG multigraph","def":"\\(\\bar G=(V,E^{\\to},E^{\\leftrightarrow},\\mathcal L,(W_\\ell,z_\\ell)_{\\ell\\in\\mathcal L})\\).","role":"systematic-selection graph","refs":["\\(V\\)","\\(E^{\\to}\\)","\\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\)","\\(\\mathcal L\\)","\\(W_\\ell\\)","\\(z_\\ell\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(\\bar G\\)","type":"labelled selection acyclic directed mixed multigraph","def":"\\(\\bar G\\) has vertex set \\(V\\), directed and bidirected edge copies \\(E^{\\to}\\) and \\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\), and child-label sets on edge copies; \\(\\bar G^{[s]}\\) deletes every edge copy whose label names a vertex in \\(J(s)\\).","role":"systematic-selection latent-projection graph","refs":["\\(V\\)","\\(E^{\\to}\\)","\\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\)","\\(J(u)\\)"]}
changed.reason: The frozen graph omitted selector-incident edges and therefore could not encode or verify the published Algorithm 2 trace.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(\\mathfrak H\\)","type":"tuple","def":"\\(\\mathfrak H=(d,y,b_L,b_R,P_L,P_R,e_\\star)\\).","role":"context-specific hedge","refs":["\\(d\\)","\\(y\\)","\\(b_L,b_R\\)","\\(P_L,P_R\\)","\\(e_\\star\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(\\mathfrak H\\)","type":"finite response-null certificate tuple","def":"\\(\\mathfrak H=(e_\\star,\\Omega_\\star,\\lambda_0,h,\\mathbf f,\\rho)\\) specifies one graph-compatible finite canonical CS-SCM response skeleton and one observed-null but target-nonnull perturbation of an edge-local exogenous law.","role":"context-specific nonidentification certificate","refs":["\\(e_\\star\\)","\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(A\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(Y\\)"]}
changed.reason: The two-arm tuple is too narrow for the exact terminal semantics; the replacement records the finite response-null object whose paired models can be checked directly.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(e_\\star\\)","type":"element of \\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\)","def":"Bidirected edge copy with endpoints \\(b_L\\) and \\(b_R\\).","role":"coupling edge","refs":["\\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\)","\\(b_L,b_R\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(e_\\star\\)","type":"element of \\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\)","def":"The distinguished bidirected edge copy whose edge-local exogenous law is perturbed; in the two-arm parity construction its endpoints are \\(b_L\\) and \\(b_R\\).","role":"distinguished coupling edge","refs":["\\(E^{\\leftrightarrow}\\)","\\(b_L,b_R\\)"]}
changed.reason: The broad response-null certificate may perturb a selector-incident edge on a minimal terminal graph, while the parity specialization still uses \(b_L\leftrightarrow b_R\).

changed.from: {"name":"\\(U_\\star\\)","type":"exogenous random variable","def":"\\(U_\\star=(B_L,B_R)\\) is an exogenous parent only of \\(b_L\\) and \\(b_R\\).","role":"factorized coupling source","refs":["\\(B_L,B_R\\)","\\(b_L,b_R\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(U_\\star\\)","type":"finite exogenous random variable","def":"The exogenous source associated with \\(e_\\star\\), parent only of the endpoints of that edge copy; in the parity specialization \\(U_\\star=(B_L,B_R)\\).","role":"distinguished edge-local source","refs":["\\(e_\\star\\)","\\(B_L,B_R\\)"]}
changed.reason: The response-null certificate needs an arbitrary finite edge-local source, whereas the supporting parity model uses its two-bit specialization.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal H_{\\mathrm{CS}}\\)","type":"class","def":"Context-specific hedges for \\((\\bar G,A,a,Y,\\mathcal U)\\).","role":"graphical certificate class","refs":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(A\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(Y\\)","\\(\\mathcal U\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal H_{\\mathrm{CS}}\\)","type":"finite-dimensional semialgebraic set","def":"The set of context-specific response-null certificate tuples for \\(\\bar G,A,a,Y,\\mathcal U\\).","role":"graph-readable paired-model certificate set","refs":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(A\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(Y\\)","\\(\\mathcal U\\)"]}
changed.reason: The class now denotes the broad finite response-function certificate set rather than a special two-arm subgraph.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(Q_\\kappa\\)","type":"subprobability law on \\(\\{0,1\\}^{Y}\\)","def":"\\(Q_\\kappa(x_Y)=p_{M_\\kappa}(X_Y(a)=x_Y,S=\\emptyset)\\).","role":"target law","refs":["\\(M_\\kappa\\)","\\(Y\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(\\emptyset\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(Q_\\kappa\\)","type":"probability law on \\(\\{0,1\\}^{Y}\\)","def":"\\(Q_\\kappa(x_Y)=p_{M_\\kappa}(X_Y(a,S=\\emptyset)=x_Y)\\).","role":"context-setting interventional target law","refs":["\\(M_\\kappa\\)","\\(Y\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(\\emptyset\\)"]}
changed.reason: The target is normalized and interventional in \(S\), not a natural-selector subprobability law.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal I\\)","type":"finite SS-ID input","def":"A graph, selector contexts, intervention, and outcome query supplied to \\(\\mathsf{SS\\text{-}ID}\\).","role":"algorithm input"}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal I\\)","type":"finite SS-ID input","def":"A finite LS-ADMMG \\(\\bar G\\), the full observed joint law \\(p(X_{V^\\circ},S)\\), a treatment value \\(a\\), and an outcome query \\(Y\\) supplied to \\(\\mathsf{SS\\text{-}ID}\\).","role":"algorithm input","refs":["\\(\\bar G\\)","\\(V^\\circ\\)","\\(S\\)","\\(a\\)","\\(Y\\)"]}
changed.reason: The published algorithm takes one coherent observed joint distribution, not an arbitrary collection of selector conditionals plus a target-context mass.

changed.from: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal C_{\\mathrm{cs2}}\\)","type":"class","def":"Inputs reaching the remaining terminal Algorithm 2 return denoted \\(\\mathtt{cs\\_fail\\_2}\\).","role":"uncovered failure class","refs":["\\(\\mathcal I\\)","\\(\\mathsf{SS\\text{-}ID}\\)"]}
changed.to: {"name":"\\(\\mathcal C_{\\mathrm{cs2}}\\)","type":"class","def":"Finite inputs whose Algorithm 2 candidate loop is exhausted and whose execution reaches the final bare failure return at line 9.","role":"uncovered terminal failure class","refs":["\\(\\mathcal I\\)","\\(\\mathsf{SS\\text{-}ID}\\)"]}
changed.reason: This distinguishes the uncovered final return from the line-3 hedge return already covered by the published nonidentification theorem.

changed.from: stale reverse dependencies
changed.to: rebuilt direct used_by inverse
changed.reason: Replace the obsolete two-arm CS-hedge node, remove all uses of \(\pi(\emptyset)\), and rebuild direct \(\mathrm{used\_by}\) inverses after the definition and statement changes.
